mnemonics: vanishing Gradients are Killing Your Model
keyPoints: Vanishing Gradients: Gradients become smaller as they backpropagate through the network, making optimization challenging.
Exploding Gradients: Large gradients can cause the model's weights to explode, leading to instability.
Dead Neurons: If the activation function is too weak, neurons may not be activated, affecting the model's performance.
Overfitting: When the model is too complex and fits the training data too well, it may not generalize well to new data.
Non-Differentiable Functions: Some functions, like the ReLU activation function, are non-differentiable, making optimization tricky.
